chapter_title: Outdoor Signs & Motorist Info. Panels on Toll Roads in Certain Counties
text:
(a) The governing body may deposit all or part of the county property tax paid on a sign, on the real property on which a sign is located, or on other real or personal property owned by the owner of a sign to the credit of a sign abatement and community beautification fund in the county treasury.

(b) The governing body may use money in the fund to compensate the owner of a sign required to be removed under this subchapter.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 6
### Chapter 395. Outdoor Signs & Motorist Info. Panels on Toll Roads in Certain Counties
##### § 395.007 — Sign Abatement and Beautification Fund

(a) The governing body may deposit all or part of the county property tax paid on a sign, on the real property on which a sign is located, or on other real or personal property owned by the owner of a sign to the credit of a sign abatement and community beautification fund in the county treasury.

(b) The governing body may use money in the fund to compensate the owner of a sign required to be removed under this subchapter.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: The governing body may deposit all or part of the county property tax paid on a sign, on the real property on which a sign is located, or on other real or personal property owned by the owner of a sign to the credit of a sign abatement and community beautification fund in the county treasury.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: The governing body may use money in the fund to compensate the owner of a sign required to be removed under this subchapter.

history.raw: Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.
